Marbella · 20–23 May 2027 · Small group · Invite-only

It’s not a holiday.Not a retreat.Not a death march.

Four days of training, summit, ocean and recovery in the mountains above Marbella — for men who’ve spent years at the bottom of their own list.

Accommodation, food and coaching included. The full itinerary stays sealed. That’s the point.

Secure my place Get the full breakdown
What this actually is

The hardest part is explaining what it is

Most challenges work like this: turn up, suffer for an hour, collect a medal, go home. This isn’t that. Four days. Real training, real adventure, real recovery. A mountain summit. The ocean. A hand-built assault course with 150+ obstacles. Ice baths and sauna. And a group of men who start as strangers and don’t stay that way.

It’s built to challenge you hard, then look after you. Pushed in a way you’d never push yourself, then brought back down to recover properly. No nasty surprises.

“It’s not a holiday, not a retreat, not a grueling death march, and not a boys’ trip, but it’s a blend of all of those. It takes the good things from each.”
— Jonny, 36 · Business owner / Personal trainer
The man leading it

This is run by Callum

Not a weekend operator. A personal trainer since 18 and a mindset and behavioural-change coach since 21, Callum has spent his career on the part of this that matters most: the inner game. He’s the performance coach behind hundreds of men, and the 2027 Challenge is his 7th edition.

He doesn’t coach from the sidelines. He’s in it, every day of it, which is exactly why men push harder here than they ever would alone.

18+Years coaching
100sOf men coached
7thEdition · 2027

More on Callum below →

Before you talk yourself out of it

“Am I fit enough for this?”

Here’s the honest answer: you need to be fit enough to train consistently, not fit enough to win anything. We meet you where you are, and the coaching builds the base in the weeks before you arrive. This is not only for the super-fit. The least fit man in the room finds the same edge as everyone else, and most men who come have never done anything like it. That’s the point.

“If you feel intimidated, or feel it’s only for people with a certain physicality, no. This is an opportunity to develop yourself physically, but also in leadership, in a team, coming together as a group of men.”
— Ben, 48 · Business owner
The work happens in your head

Everyone arrives thinking it’s physical

They leave talking about their minds. That’s the thread through every single man who’s done it. The least fit man in the room and the one who’s plateaued both find the same edge.

“I struggled with the 55 the first couple of days, so picking a 70 off the floor seemed impossible. But I went in believing it would happen, and the body just responded. If the mind can see it, the body finds a way.”
— Vinny, 44 · Father / Shift worker
You don’t do it alone

The group is what makes the impossible possible

Nobody gets through this on their own, and that’s by design. The bonds are what men talk about months later. You arrive a stranger; you don’t leave as one.

“When you see your teammates suffering as much as you are, some even more, it’s like, what’s your excuse?”
— Ziggy, 41 · Father / Business owner
Not just suffering

Summit, gorge, open water

It’s a challenge, not a punishment. Between the hard sessions there’s real adventure — a mountain summit, hidden gorges, a session in the ocean. The range is the point: you’re pushed, then you’re somewhere most men never get to stand.

The real objection

“I can’t justify the time”

Every responsible man feels it. Here’s the answer, from men who run businesses and raise kids and felt exactly the same.

“It’s an investment in yourself. If you just take, take, take, the cup gets empty. This filled my cup. I’m exhausted physically, but mentally my cup is full now, and I can pour out to others.”
— Ziggy
“Even if I’m now giving 50% where I used to give 100, I’m giving 50% of a very full pot, so everyone wins. It’s almost more selfless to become selfish.”
— Vinny
“I’ve missed my little girl like crazy. But she’ll get a better version of me when I go back. Your kids shine a mirror back at your own challenges.”
— Sam
“The trip pays for itself because of who you become. After that, goals aren’t about ticking boxes, they’re about becoming the version of you that makes them inevitable.”
— Jonny
What you take home

It doesn’t stay on the mountain

Leadership, decision-making under pressure, the way you show up at work and at home — men consistently say the change shows up in real life. And the reason they push harder here than they ever would alone is the coaching.

“I’ve never experienced a coach with the ability to lead people through very difficult challenges but not break them. He knows their pace, what pushes them to the edge, and takes them to a place where they grow.”
— Ben · on Callum
A program, not just an event

The coaching starts the day you sign up, not the day you arrive

From the moment you’re in, you’re in the group: WhatsApp coaching and the Wildman app, supporting your training all the way to the event. The earlier you commit, the more runway you get, and the better shape you arrive in.

Before

1 · Sign up

You commit. The clock starts in your favour — the earlier you’re in, the more runway you get.

Before

2 · Start training

Straight into the group, the app, the work. Supported from day one.

Before

3 · Get coached up

Built into the best physical and mental shape you can arrive in.

The four days

4 · The event

Find your strengths. Face your weaknesses. Challenge yourself past the point where you’d normally stop. Raise your standards.

After

5 · Bring it home

Implement what you learned about yourself into your world — the value pours back out, to work, to family, to how you lead. A different man. Not the one who signed up.

Built by someone who’s been in it

Meet Callum

Founder & coach · Wildman Challenge

Callum isn’t just a fitness coach. A personal trainer since 18 and a mindset and behavioural-change coach since 21, he’s spent his career on the part of this that actually matters — the inner game. He’s been the performance coach to hundreds of men: dads, husbands, and founders of six-, seven- and eight-figure businesses. For over a decade he’s also led a mindset academy, certifying coaches across every discipline in the psychology he uses on the mountain.

That’s why men push harder here than they ever would alone. He knows the exact line between the edge and breaking, and keeps you on the right side of it. He doesn’t coach from the sidelines. He’s in it, every day of it.

18+Years coaching

Challenged hard, then looked after

No nasty surprises

Every push has a cool-down built in. Sauna, ice baths, proper recovery — you’re taken to the edge in a way you’d never take yourself, then brought back down. The structure does what you won’t do alone, safely.

All of it hosted at a world-class facility in the hills above Marbella — the kind of place athletes fly in for. The standard of the facility is the standard of everything else.

“You get challenged and pushed in a manageable way, then there’s a cool-down. No nasty surprises.”
— Jonny
Why not just do a race?

A race is an afternoon. This is four days.

The depth is the difference, and the men who say so have done the races.

“I’d done the Spartan, and in comparison that was nothing. Spartan was a nice warm-up. This was the gear change I was after.”
— Vinny
Read this first

Who this isn’t for

If you want a holiday with a few workouts attached, this isn’t it. And if you want to skip the months of coaching and just turn up for the four days — that’s not on offer. The preparation is the product. The event is the test.

Pricing & how to secure your place

The earlier you’re in, the more runway you get — and the less you pay

FROM £2,250
  • Accommodation
  • All food
  • The four-day Challenge
  • Coaching from sign-up — WhatsApp + app
  • Recovery — sauna, ice baths

Not included: flights. Fly into Málaga — base is about 45 minutes away.

£250 secures your place. Spread the rest monthly — finished before you fly, set up with us after you’re in. The earlier you’re in, the lighter the months.

Commit early and you get more coaching runway before Marbella — and a lower price. Spots are limited and the group fills.

Not sure yet? Book a call first, no pressure. We’ll talk it through honestly and you decide if it’s right for you.

Coming with others?

A lot of men do this alongside people they trust, and it lands harder when you do. If you’re thinking of bringing others, there’s a group rate available — just mention it on your call or message us and we’ll talk it through.

Before you ask

The questions that stop men booking

How fit do I need to be?

Fit enough to train consistently. We meet you where you are — the coaching builds the base in the weeks before you arrive. The least fit man in the room finds the same edge as everyone else.

What if I’ve never done anything like this?

Good. Most haven’t. You won’t know everything that’s coming — that’s by design. You don’t need experience; you need to show up.

Why is it more than a race?

A race is an afternoon. This is four days plus weeks of coaching before you arrive. The depth is the difference — ask any of the men who’ve done both.

What’s the deposit?

£250 secures your place. The earlier you’re in, the more coaching runway you get before Marbella.

Can I just do the event without the programme?

No. The preparation is the product; the event is the test. There’s one version of this, and it starts the day you sign up.

How does paying monthly work?

£250 secures your place. The rest is spread monthly and finished before you fly — we set your plan up with you after you’re in. The earlier you join, the lighter the months.

What happens when you commit

The four days are the test. The months before are how you pass.

This isn’t a ticket you buy and turn up to. The day your deposit clears, the programme starts. You’re in the group, in the app, and the coaching begins — building you physically and mentally toward Marbella.

The moment you’re in
Deposit paid, place held. You join the Wildman group and the app that day.
The weeks that follow
Coaching, programming and support — getting you into the strongest shape of your life, body and mind, before you set foot on the mountain.
Marbella
You don’t arrive hoping to survive it. You arrive ready to meet it, and find out what you’re made of.
After
You take the man you became back into your work, your family, your life.

This is why the men who commit early get the most from it. Whenever you start, the event is the same four days, but how much you get out of them is decided long before you arrive. The earlier you begin, the more of yourself you bring to the mountain.

The close

If you’re sitting in the haze of do I, don’t I

“If you want to stay asleep and stay in your comfort zone, don’t. But if you want to grow in every aspect of your life and see what you’re really made of, then join. It’s going to be challenging. It’s going to be painful. But I promise you it’s going to pay off.”
— Fran, 33 · Business owner
